task.title: Publish API Documentation
task.shortdesc: Publish your Postman collection documentation to a publicly accessible URL so that API consumers can view the documentation in a browser without requiring a Postman account.

prereq.p: Before you publish documentation, ensure that:

ul.li: You have reviewed the documentation preview and confirmed it is complete. For instructions, see Generate Documentation.
You are signed in to a Postman account with the collection saved to a workspace.

context.p: Publishing documentation in Postman creates a hosted web page at a Postman-generated URL. You can share this URL with developers, add it to your developer portal, or link it from a README file. Published documentation updates automatically when you make changes to your collection.

step.cmd: In the documentation preview panel, select Publish in the upper-right corner.
In the Environment dropdown, select the environment whose variable values Postman will use in the published documentation.

note.p: Postman replaces variables in the published documentation with their current values from the selected environment. Do not select an environment that contains sensitive values such as API keys or passwords. Use a dedicated documentation environment with sanitized values.

step.cmd: Under Visibility, select Public to allow anyone with the URL to view the documentation.
Review the generated URL displayed in the Documentation URL field.

note.p: To use a custom domain, see .

step.stepresult: Postman publishes the documentation and displays a confirmation dialog with the public URL.

step.cmd: Select View documentation to open the published page in your browser and verify the content.
Copy the URL and share it with your API consumers.

r.p: The API documentation is published and accessible at the displayed URL. The page updates automatically each time you edit the collection and republish. Viewers do not need a Postman account to access public documentation.

postreq.p: Optionally, customize the documentation with a logo, color scheme, and custom domain. For instructions, see Customize Documentation.
